     1‚Üípackage markdown
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"fmt"
     5‚Üí	"sort"
     6‚Üí	"strings"
     7‚Üí	"time"
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
    10‚Üí)
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí// Type aliases for convenience - use the shared schema types
    13‚Üítype (
    14‚Üí	SessionData  = schema.SessionData
    15‚Üí	ProviderInfo = schema.ProviderInfo
    16‚Üí	Exchange     = schema.Exchange
    17‚Üí	Message      = schema.Message
    18‚Üí	ContentPart  = schema.ContentPart
    19‚Üí	ToolInfo     = schema.ToolInfo
    20‚Üí)
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí// Markdown constants
    23‚Üíconst (
    24‚Üí	GeneratedBySpecStory = "<!-- Generated by SpecStory, Markdown v2.1.0 -->"
    25‚Üí	ToolUseOpenTag       = "<tool-use data-tool-type=\"%s\" data-tool-name=\"%s\"><details>"
    26‚Üí	ToolUseCloseTag      = "</details></tool-use>"
    27‚Üí)
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí// GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession generates markdown from a SessionData
    30‚Üí// This is provider-agnostic and works from the unified agent session JSON format
    31‚Üí// If includeMessageIDs is true, message IDs will be included in the output
    32‚Üí// useUTC controls timestamp format (true=UTC, false=local)
    33‚Üífunc GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(sessionData *SessionData, includeMessageIDs bool, useUTC bool) (string, error) {
    34‚Üí	if sessionData == nil {
    35‚Üí		return "", fmt.Errorf("sessionData is nil")
    36‚Üí	}
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí	var markdown strings.Builder
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí	// Add header comment
    41‚Üí	markdown.WriteString(GeneratedBySpecStory + "\n\n")
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí	// Create title from session info
    44‚Üí	title := generateTitle(sessionData, useUTC)
    45‚Üí	fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "# %s\n\n", title)
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí	// Add session metadata comment using the same timestamp format as the title
    48‚Üí	var commentTimestamp string
    49‚Üí	if len(sessionData.Exchanges) > 0 && sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime != "" {
    50‚Üí		commentTimestamp = formatTimestamp(sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime, useUTC)
    51‚Üí	} else {
    52‚Üí		commentTimestamp = formatTimestamp(sessionData.CreatedAt, useUTC)
    53‚Üí	}
    54‚Üí	fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<!-- %s Session %s (%s) -->\n\n",
    55‚Üí		sessionData.Provider.Name,
    56‚Üí		sessionData.SessionID,
    57‚Üí		commentTimestamp)
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí	// Render each exchange, tracking role across exchanges for proper separators
    60‚Üí	prevRole := ""
    61‚Üí	for _, exchange := range sessionData.Exchanges {
    62‚Üí		for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
    63‚Üí			markdown.WriteString(renderMessage(msg, prevRole, includeMessageIDs, useUTC))
    64‚Üí			prevRole = msg.Role
    65‚Üí		}
    66‚Üí	}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí	// Ensure markdown ends with exactly one newline
    69‚Üí	result := strings.TrimRight(markdown.String(), "\n") + "\n"
    70‚Üí	return result, nil
    71‚Üí}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí// generateTitle creates a title for the session
    74‚Üífunc generateTitle(sessionData *SessionData, useUTC bool) string {
    75‚Üí	// Use first exchange start time for the title (matching existing markdown format)
    76‚Üí	// This is the timestamp of the first user message
    77‚Üí	if len(sessionData.Exchanges) > 0 && sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime != "" {
    78‚Üí		return formatTimestamp(sessionData.Exchanges[0].StartTime, useUTC)
    79‚Üí	}
    80‚Üí	// Fallback to session creation time if no exchanges or no start time
    81‚Üí	return formatTimestamp(sessionData.CreatedAt, useUTC)
    82‚Üí}
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí// formatTimestamp formats an ISO 8601 timestamp for display in title and comments
    85‚Üífunc formatTimestamp(timestamp string, useUTC bool) string {
    86‚Üí	t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timestamp)
    87‚Üí	if err != nil {
    88‚Üí		return timestamp
    89‚Üí	}
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí	if useUTC {
    92‚Üí		// Use UTC with Z suffix (default): "2025-11-13 21:12:14Z"
    93‚Üí		return t.UTC().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "Z"
    94‚Üí	}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí	// Use local timezone with offset: "2025-11-13 21:12:14-0700"
    97‚Üí	return t.Local().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05-0700")
    98‚Üí}
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí// renderMessage renders a single message with role header, content, and optional tool use
   101‚Üífunc renderMessage(msg Message, prevRole string, includeMessageIDs bool, useUTC bool) string {
   102‚Üí	var markdown strings.Builder
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí	// Add separator before this message if role changed from previous message
   105‚Üí	if shouldAddSeparator(msg.Role, prevRole) {
   106‚Üí		markdown.WriteString("---\n\n")
   107‚Üí	}
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí	// Add role header
   110‚Üí	roleHeader := renderRoleHeader(msg, useUTC)
   111‚Üí	if includeMessageIDs && msg.ID != "" {
   112‚Üí		// Strip one trailing newline so the message ID comment appears on the next line
   113‚Üí		roleHeader = strings.TrimSuffix(roleHeader, "\n")
   114‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(roleHeader)
   115‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<!-- message-id: %s -->\n\n", msg.ID)
   116‚Üí	} else {
   117‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(roleHeader)
   118‚Üí	}
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí	// Render content parts (already includes trailing newlines)
   121‚Üí	if len(msg.Content) > 0 {
   122‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(renderContentParts(msg.Content))
   123‚Üí	}
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí	// Render tool use if present
   126‚Üí	if msg.Tool != nil {
   127‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(renderToolInfo(msg.Tool))
   128‚Üí		markdown.WriteString("\n")
   129‚Üí	}
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí	return markdown.String()
   132‚Üí}
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí// renderRoleHeader creates the role header for a message
   135‚Üífunc renderRoleHeader(msg Message, useUTC bool) string {
   136‚Üí	// Check if this is a sidechain message (subagent conversation)
   137‚Üí	sidechainMarker := ""
   138‚Üí	if isSidechain, ok := msg.Metadata["isSidechain"].(bool); ok && isSidechain {
   139‚Üí		sidechainMarker = " - sidechain"
   140‚Üí	}
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí	if msg.Role == "user" {
   143‚Üí		// User message - include timestamp if available
   144‚Üí		if msg.Timestamp != "" {
   145‚Üí			formattedTimestamp := formatTimestamp(msg.Timestamp, useUTC)
   146‚Üí			return fmt.Sprintf("_**User%s (%s)**_\n\n", sidechainMarker, formattedTimestamp)
   147‚Üí		}
   148‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("_**User%s**_\n\n", sidechainMarker)
   149‚Üí	}
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí	// Agent message - include model and timestamp if available
   152‚Üí	if msg.Model != "" && msg.Timestamp != "" {
   153‚Üí		formattedTimestamp := formatTimestamp(msg.Timestamp, useUTC)
   154‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("_**Agent%s (%s %s)**_\n\n", sidechainMarker, msg.Model, formattedTimestamp)
   155‚Üí	}
   156‚Üí	if msg.Model != "" {
   157‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("_**Agent%s (%s)**_\n\n", sidechainMarker, msg.Model)
   158‚Üí	}
   159‚Üí	if msg.Timestamp != "" {
   160‚Üí		formattedTimestamp := formatTimestamp(msg.Timestamp, useUTC)
   161‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("_**Agent%s (%s)**_\n\n", sidechainMarker, formattedTimestamp)
   162‚Üí	}
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("_**Agent%s**_\n\n", sidechainMarker)
   165‚Üí}
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí// renderContentParts renders all content parts in a message
   168‚Üífunc renderContentParts(parts []ContentPart) string {
   169‚Üí	var markdown strings.Builder
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí	for _, part := range parts {
   172‚Üí		switch part.Type {
   173‚Üí		case "text":
   174‚Üí			// Regular text content
   175‚Üí			markdown.WriteString(part.Text)
   176‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("\n\n")
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí		case "thinking":
   179‚Üí			// Thinking content in collapsible block
   180‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>\n")
   181‚Üí			markdown.WriteString(part.Text)
   182‚Üí			// Only add newline before closing tag if text doesn't already end with one
   183‚Üí			if !strings.HasSuffix(part.Text, "\n") {
   184‚Üí				markdown.WriteString("\n")
   185‚Üí			}
   186‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("</details></think>\n\n")
   187‚Üí		}
   188‚Üí	}
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí	return markdown.String()
   191‚Üí}
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí// renderToolInfo renders tool use information
   194‚Üífunc renderToolInfo(tool *ToolInfo) string {
   195‚Üí	var markdown strings.Builder
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí	// Get the tool type for the data attribute
   198‚Üí	toolType := tool.Type
   199‚Üí	if toolType == "" {
   200‚Üí		toolType = "generic"
   201‚Üí	}
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí	// Open tool-use tag
   204‚Üí	fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, ToolUseOpenTag, toolType, tool.Name)
   205‚Üí	markdown.WriteString("\n")
   206‚Üí
   207‚Üí	// Generate summary tag
   208‚Üí	if tool.Summary != nil && *tool.Summary != "" {
   209‚Üí		// Provider provided custom summary content
   210‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<summary>%s</summary>\n", *tool.Summary)
   211‚Üí	} else {
   212‚Üí		// Generate default summary
   213‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "<summary>Tool use: **%s**</summary>\n", tool.Name)
   214‚Üí	}
   215‚Üí
   216‚Üí	// Use pre-formatted markdown from provider if present
   217‚Üí	if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil && *tool.FormattedMarkdown != "" {
   218‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(*tool.FormattedMarkdown)
   219‚Üí		// Ensure there's a newline before the closing tag
   220‚Üí		// Some providers include it, some don't - normalize here
   221‚Üí		if !strings.HasSuffix(*tool.FormattedMarkdown, "\n") {
   222‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("\n")
   223‚Üí		}
   224‚Üí	} else {
   225‚Üí		// Fallback to generic formatting
   226‚Üí		markdown.WriteString(renderGenericTool(tool))
   227‚Üí	}
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí	// Close tool-use tag
   230‚Üí	markdown.WriteString(ToolUseCloseTag)
   231‚Üí	markdown.WriteString("\n")
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí	return markdown.String()
   234‚Üí}
   235‚Üí
   236‚Üí// renderGenericTool renders a tool without provider-specific formatting
   237‚Üí// Displays all available information as key-value pairs
   238‚Üí// Note: The <summary> tag is already written by the caller (renderToolMessage)
   239‚Üífunc renderGenericTool(tool *ToolInfo) string {
   240‚Üí	var markdown strings.Builder
   241‚Üí	markdown.WriteString("\n")
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí	// Display input as key-value pairs if present
   244‚Üí	// Sort keys for deterministic output order
   245‚Üí	if len(tool.Input) > 0 {
   246‚Üí		markdown.WriteString("**Input:**\n\n")
   247‚Üí
   248‚Üí		// Extract and sort keys for deterministic ordering
   249‚Üí		keys := make([]string, 0, len(tool.Input))
   250‚Üí		for key := range tool.Input {
   251‚Üí			// Skip internal fields like _cwd
   252‚Üí			if !strings.HasPrefix(key, "_") {
   253‚Üí				keys = append(keys, key)
   254‚Üí			}
   255‚Üí		}
   256‚Üí		sort.Strings(keys)
   257‚Üí
   258‚Üí		for _, key := range keys {
   259‚Üí			fmt.Fprintf(&markdown, "- %s: `%v`\n", key, tool.Input[key])
   260‚Üí		}
   261‚Üí		markdown.WriteString("\n")
   262‚Üí	}
   263‚Üí
   264‚Üí	// Display output if present
   265‚Üí	if tool.Output != nil {
   266‚Üí		if isError, ok := tool.Output["is_error"].(bool); ok && isError {
   267‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("**Error:**\n\n")
   268‚Üí		} else {
   269‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("**Result:**\n\n")
   270‚Üí		}
   271‚Üí
   272‚Üí		if content, ok := tool.Output["content"].(string); ok {
   273‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("```\n")
   274‚Üí			markdown.WriteString(content)
   275‚Üí			markdown.WriteString("\n```\n")
   276‚Üí		}
   277‚Üí	}
   278‚Üí
   279‚Üí	return markdown.String()
   280‚Üí}
   281‚Üí
   282‚Üí// shouldAddSeparator determines if a separator should be added between messages
   283‚Üífunc shouldAddSeparator(currentRole, prevRole string) bool {
   284‚Üí	// Add separator on role transitions
   285‚Üí	if prevRole == "" {
   286‚Üí		return false
   287‚Üí	}
   288‚Üí	return currentRole != prevRole
   289‚Üí}
   290‚Üí

     7‚Üí
     8‚Üífunc TestFormatTimestamp(t *testing.T) {

    10‚Üí		name      string
    11‚Üí		timestamp string
    12‚Üí		useUTC    bool
    13‚Üí		expected  string

    18‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    19‚Üí			expected:  "2026-01-25 15:30:45Z",
    20‚Üí		},
    21‚Üí		{

    23‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T17:30:45+02:00",
    24‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    25‚Üí			expected:  "2026-01-25 15:30:45Z", // +02:00 converted to UTC
    26‚Üí		},
    27‚Üí		{
    28‚Üí			name:      "valid RFC3339 with negative offset - useUTC true converts to UTC",
    29‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T08:30:45-07:00",
    30‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    31‚Üí			expected:  "2026-01-25 15:30:45Z", // -07:00 converted to UTC
    32‚Üí		},
    33‚Üí		{
    34‚Üí			name:      "midnight UTC",
    35‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T00:00:00Z",
    36‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    37‚Üí			expected:  "2026-01-25 00:00:00Z",
    38‚Üí		},
    39‚Üí		{
    40‚Üí			name:      "end of day UTC",
    41‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T23:59:59Z",
    42‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    43‚Üí			expected:  "2026-01-25 23:59:59Z",
    44‚Üí		},
    45‚Üí		{
    46‚Üí			name:      "leap year date",
    47‚Üí			timestamp: "2024-02-29T12:00:00Z",
    48‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    49‚Üí			expected:  "2024-02-29 12:00:00Z",
    50‚Üí		},
    51‚Üí		{
    52‚Üí			name:      "invalid timestamp - returns original",
    53‚Üí			timestamp: "not-a-timestamp",
    54‚Üí			useUTC:    true,
    55‚Üí			expected:  "not-a-timestamp",
    56‚Üí		},
    57‚Üí		{

    87‚Üí
    88‚Üífunc TestFormatTimestamp_LocalTimezone(t *testing.T) {
    89‚Üí	// Compute expected output using the same logic as the function under test.
    90‚Üí	// This makes the test deterministic regardless of machine timezone.
    91‚Üí	tests := []struct {
    92‚Üí		name      string
    93‚Üí		timestamp string
    94‚Üí	}{
    95‚Üí		{
    96‚Üí			name:      "valid timestamp produces offset format",
    97‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T15:30:45Z",
    98‚Üí		},
    99‚Üí		{
   100‚Üí			name:      "timestamp with offset",
   101‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T08:30:45-07:00",
   102‚Üí		},
   103‚Üí		{
   104‚Üí			name:      "timestamp with milliseconds - milliseconds stripped",
   105‚Üí			timestamp: "2026-01-25T15:30:45.123Z",
   106‚Üí		},
   107‚Üí	}
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
   110‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   111‚Üí			// Compute expected value by parsing and formatting in local timezone
   112‚Üí			parsed, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, tt.timestamp)
   113‚Üí			expected := parsed.Local().Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05-0700")
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí			result := formatTimestamp(tt.timestamp, false)
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí			if result != expected {
   118‚Üí				t.Errorf("formatTimestamp(%q, false) = %q, want %q",
   119‚Üí					tt.timestamp, result, expected)
   120‚Üí			}
   121‚Üí		})
   122‚Üí	}
   123‚Üí}
   124‚Üí

     1‚Üípackage geminicli
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"encoding/json"
     5‚Üí	"fmt"
     6‚Üí	"path/filepath"
     7‚Üí	"strings"
     8‚Üí)
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí// formatToolAsMarkdown generates formatted markdown for a ToolInfo (input + output)
    11‚Üí// Returns the inner content only (no <tool-use> tags - those are added by pkg/markdown)
    12‚Üí// Also sets tool.Summary if a custom summary is needed
    13‚Üífunc formatToolAsMarkdown(tool *ToolInfo) string {
    14‚Üí	if tool == nil {
    15‚Üí		return ""
    16‚Üí	}
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí	// Special case for referenced_files - content is already formatted in agent_session.go
    19‚Üí	if tool.Name == "referenced_files" {
    20‚Üí		if tool.FormattedMarkdown != nil {
    21‚Üí			return *tool.FormattedMarkdown
    22‚Üí		}
    23‚Üí		return ""
    24‚Üí	}
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí	// Build custom summary for certain tools (appending key parameters)
    27‚Üí	var customSummary string
    28‚Üí	switch tool.Name {
    29‚Üí	case "read_file":
    30‚Üí		if filePath := inputAsString(tool.Input, "file_path"); filePath != "" {
    31‚Üí			customSummary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** `%s`", tool.Name, filePath)
    32‚Üí		}
    33‚Üí	case "search_file_content":
    34‚Üí		pattern := inputAsString(tool.Input, "pattern")
    35‚Üí		include := inputAsString(tool.Input, "include")
    36‚Üí		if pattern != "" {
    37‚Üí			if include != "" {
    38‚Üí				customSummary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** `%s` in `%s`", tool.Name, pattern, include)
    39‚Üí			} else {
    40‚Üí				customSummary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** `%s`", tool.Name, pattern)
    41‚Üí			}
    42‚Üí		}
    43‚Üí	case "glob":
    44‚Üí		if pattern := inputAsString(tool.Input, "pattern"); pattern != "" {
    45‚Üí			customSummary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** `%s`", tool.Name, pattern)
    46‚Üí		}
    47‚Üí	case "list_directory":
    48‚Üí		if dirPath := inputAsString(tool.Input, "dir_path"); dirPath != "" {
    49‚Üí			customSummary = fmt.Sprintf("Tool use: **%s** `%s`", tool.Name, dirPath)
    50‚Üí		}
    51‚Üí	}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí	// Set custom summary on tool if we built one
    54‚Üí	if customSummary != "" {
    55‚Üí		tool.Summary = &customSummary
    56‚Üí	}
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí	// Build body content only (no wrapper tags)
    59‚Üí	body := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolBodyFromInput(tool))
    60‚Üí	result := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolResultFromOutput(tool))
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí	var builder strings.Builder
    63‚Üí	if body != "" {
    64‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n")
    65‚Üí		builder.WriteString(body)
    66‚Üí	}
    67‚Üí	if result != "" {
    68‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n\n")
    69‚Üí		builder.WriteString(result)
    70‚Üí	}
    71‚Üí	if builder.Len() > 0 {
    72‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n")
    73‚Üí	}
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí	return builder.String()
    76‚Üí}
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí// formatToolBodyFromInput formats the tool input/body section
    79‚Üífunc formatToolBodyFromInput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
    80‚Üí	switch tool.Name {
    81‚Üí	case "run_shell_command":
    82‚Üí		return formatShellBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    83‚Üí	case "write_file":
    84‚Üí		return formatWriteFileBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    85‚Üí	case "replace", "smart_edit":
    86‚Üí		return formatReplaceBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    87‚Üí	case "google_web_search":
    88‚Üí		return formatSearchBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    89‚Üí	case "web_fetch":
    90‚Üí		return formatWebFetchBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    91‚Üí	case "write_todos":
    92‚Üí		return formatTodoBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    93‚Üí	case "read_file", "search_file_content", "glob", "list_directory":
    94‚Üí		// Don't show input args - parameters are in the summary
    95‚Üí		return ""
    96‚Üí	default:
    97‚Üí		return formatGenericBodyFromInput(tool.Input)
    98‚Üí	}
    99‚Üí}
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí// formatToolResultFromOutput formats the tool result/output section
   102‚Üífunc formatToolResultFromOutput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
   103‚Üí	// Some tools display all their info in the body, so skip the result section
   104‚Üí	switch tool.Name {
   105‚Üí	case "write_todos":
   106‚Üí		return ""
   107‚Üí	case "read_file":
   108‚Üí		return formatReadFileResultFromOutput(tool)
   109‚Üí	case "search_file_content", "glob", "list_directory":
   110‚Üí		return formatSearchListResultFromOutput(tool.Output)
   111‚Üí	}
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí	// Default behavior: show output content
   114‚Üí	return formatDefaultResultFromOutput(tool.Output)
   115‚Üí}
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí// formatReadFileResultFromOutput shows file content with syntax highlighting
   118‚Üífunc formatReadFileResultFromOutput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
   119‚Üí	output := outputAsString(tool.Output)
   120‚Üí	if output == "" {
   121‚Üí		return ""
   122‚Üí	}
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí	filePath := inputAsString(tool.Input, "file_path")
   125‚Üí	lang := languageFromPath(filePath)
   126‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("```%s\n%s\n```", lang, output)
   127‚Üí}
   128‚Üí
   129‚Üí// formatSearchListResultFromOutput shows raw output without code fence
   130‚Üífunc formatSearchListResultFromOutput(output map[string]interface{}) string {
   131‚Üí	content := outputAsString(output)
   132‚Üí	if content == "" {
   133‚Üí		return ""
   134‚Üí	}
   135‚Üí	return content
   136‚Üí}
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí// formatDefaultResultFromOutput builds result with "Result:" prefix
   139‚Üífunc formatDefaultResultFromOutput(output map[string]interface{}) string {
   140‚Üí	content := outputAsString(output)
   141‚Üí	if content == "" {
   142‚Üí		return ""
   143‚Üí	}
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí	// Wrap multi-line output in code fence
   146‚Üí	formatted := formatOutputText(content)
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí	// Add "Result:" prefix
   149‚Üí	return addResultPrefix(formatted)
   150‚Üí}
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí// formatOutputText wraps multi-line output in code fence, leaves single-line as-is
   153‚Üífunc formatOutputText(output string) string {
   154‚Üí	if strings.Contains(output, "\n") {
   155‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("```text\n%s\n```", output)
   156‚Üí	}
   157‚Üí	return output
   158‚Üí}
   159‚Üí
   160‚Üí// addResultPrefix adds "Result:" or "Result:\n" depending on content format
   161‚Üífunc addResultPrefix(content string) string {
   162‚Üí	if strings.Contains(content, "\n") {
   163‚Üí		return fmt.Sprintf("Result:\n%s", content)
   164‚Üí	}
   165‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("Result: %s", content)
   166‚Üí}
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üífunc formatShellBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   169‚Üí	command := inputAsString(input, "command")
   170‚Üí	dir := inputAsString(input, "dir_path")
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí	if command == "" && dir == "" {
   173‚Üí		return ""
   174‚Üí	}
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí	var builder strings.Builder
   177‚Üí	if dir != "" {
   178‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Directory: `%s`\n\n", dir)
   179‚Üí	}
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí	if command != "" {
   182‚Üí		builder.WriteString("```bash\n")
   183‚Üí		builder.WriteString(command)
   184‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n```")
   185‚Üí	}
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üí	return builder.String()
   188‚Üí}
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üífunc formatWriteFileBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   191‚Üí	path := inputAsString(input, "file_path")
   192‚Üí	content := inputAsString(input, "content")
   193‚Üí	if path == "" && content == "" {
   194‚Üí		return ""
   195‚Üí	}
   196‚Üí
   197‚Üí	var builder strings.Builder
   198‚Üí	if path != "" {
   199‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Path: `%s`\n\n", path)
   200‚Üí	}
   201‚Üí	if content != "" {
   202‚Üí		builder.WriteString("```")
   203‚Üí		builder.WriteString(languageFromPath(path))
   204‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n")
   205‚Üí		builder.WriteString(content)
   206‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n```")
   207‚Üí	}
   208‚Üí	return builder.String()
   209‚Üí}
   210‚Üí
   211‚Üífunc formatReplaceBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   212‚Üí	path := inputAsString(input, "file_path")
   213‚Üí	newString := inputAsString(input, "new_string")
   214‚Üí	if path == "" && newString == "" {
   215‚Üí		return ""
   216‚Üí	}
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí	var builder strings.Builder
   219‚Üí	if path != "" {
   220‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Path: `%s`\n\n", path)
   221‚Üí	}
   222‚Üí	if newString != "" {
   223‚Üí		builder.WriteString("```diff\n")
   224‚Üí		builder.WriteString(truncate(newString, 2000))
   225‚Üí		builder.WriteString("\n```")
   226‚Üí	}
   227‚Üí	return builder.String()
   228‚Üí}
   229‚Üí
   230‚Üífunc formatSearchBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   231‚Üí	query := inputAsString(input, "query")
   232‚Üí	if query == "" {
   233‚Üí		return ""
   234‚Üí	}
   235‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("Query: %s", query)
   236‚Üí}
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üífunc formatWebFetchBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   239‚Üí	prompt := inputAsString(input, "prompt")
   240‚Üí	if prompt == "" {
   241‚Üí		return ""
   242‚Üí	}
   243‚Üí	// Show the prompt directly without a "Query:" or "Prompt:" prefix
   244‚Üí	return prompt
   245‚Üí}
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üífunc formatTodoBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   248‚Üí	todos, ok := input["todos"].([]interface{})
   249‚Üí	if !ok || len(todos) == 0 {
   250‚Üí		return ""
   251‚Üí	}
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí	var builder strings.Builder
   254‚Üí	builder.WriteString("Todo List:\n")
   255‚Üí	for _, raw := range todos {
   256‚Üí		if todo, ok := raw.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
   257‚Üí			desc, _ := todo["description"].(string)
   258‚Üí			status, _ := todo["status"].(string)
   259‚Üí			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "- [%s] %s\n", todoStatusSymbol(status), strings.TrimSpace(desc))
   260‚Üí		}
   261‚Üí	}
   262‚Üí	return builder.String()
   263‚Üí}
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üífunc todoStatusSymbol(status string) string {
   266‚Üí	switch status {
   267‚Üí	case "completed":
   268‚Üí		return "x"
   269‚Üí	case "in_progress":
   270‚Üí		return "‚ö°"
   271‚Üí	default:
   272‚Üí		return " "
   273‚Üí	}
   274‚Üí}
   275‚Üí
   276‚Üífunc formatGenericBodyFromInput(input map[string]interface{}) string {
   277‚Üí	if len(input) == 0 {
   278‚Üí		return ""
   279‚Üí	}
   280‚Üí	bytes, err := json.MarshalIndent(input, "", "  ")
   281‚Üí	if err != nil {
   282‚Üí		return ""
   283‚Üí	}
   284‚Üí	return fmt.Sprintf("```json\n%s\n```", string(bytes))
   285‚Üí}
   286‚Üí
   287‚Üí// inputAsString extracts a string value from tool input
   288‚Üífunc inputAsString(input map[string]interface{}, key string) string {
   289‚Üí	if input == nil {
   290‚Üí		return ""
   291‚Üí	}
   292‚Üí	val, ok := input[key]
   293‚Üí	if !ok {
   294‚Üí		return ""
   295‚Üí	}
   296‚Üí	switch v := val.(type) {
   297‚Üí	case string:
   298‚Üí		return v
   299‚Üí	case fmt.Stringer:
   300‚Üí		return v.String()
   301‚Üí	case []byte:
   302‚Üí		return string(v)
   303‚Üí	default:
   304‚Üí		bytes, err := json.Marshal(v)
   305‚Üí		if err != nil {
   306‚Üí			return fmt.Sprint(v)
   307‚Üí		}
   308‚Üí		return string(bytes)
   309‚Üí	}
   310‚Üí}
   311‚Üí
   312‚Üí// outputAsString extracts the output string from tool output
   313‚Üífunc outputAsString(output map[string]interface{}) string {
   314‚Üí	if output == nil {
   315‚Üí		return ""
   316‚Üí	}
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí	// Try "output" field first (common pattern)
   319‚Üí	if out, ok := output["output"].(string); ok && strings.TrimSpace(out) != "" {
   320‚Üí		return strings.TrimSpace(out)
   321‚Üí	}
   322‚Üí
   323‚Üí	// Try "content" field
   324‚Üí	if content, ok := output["content"].(string); ok && strings.TrimSpace(content) != "" {
   325‚Üí		return strings.TrimSpace(content)
   326‚Üí	}
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí	// Try "error" field
   329‚Üí	if errStr, ok := output["error"].(string); ok && strings.TrimSpace(errStr) != "" {
   330‚Üí		return strings.TrimSpace(errStr)
   331‚Üí	}
   332‚Üí
   333‚Üí	return ""
   334‚Üí}
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üífunc languageFromPath(path string) string {
   337‚Üí	if path == "" {
   338‚Üí		return "text"
   339‚Üí	}
   340‚Üí	ext := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)), ".")
   341‚Üí	if ext == "" {
   342‚Üí		return "text"
   343‚Üí	}
   344‚Üí	return ext
   345‚Üí}
   346‚Üí
   347‚Üífunc truncate(text string, limit int) string {
   348‚Üí	if limit <= 0 {
   349‚Üí		return text
   350‚Üí	}
   351‚Üí	runes := []rune(text)
   352‚Üí	if len(runes) <= limit {
   353‚Üí		return text
   354‚Üí	}
   355‚Üí	return string(runes[:limit]) + "\n... (truncated)"
   356‚Üí}
   357‚Üí
```

     1‚Üípackage geminicli

     5‚Üí	"testing"
     6‚Üí)
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üífunc TestFormatToolAsMarkdownShell(t *testing.T) {
     9‚Üí	tool := &ToolInfo{
    10‚Üí		Name:  "run_shell_command",
    11‚Üí		Type:  "shell",
    12‚Üí		UseID: "tool-1",
    13‚Üí		Input: map[string]interface{}{"command": "ls -la"},
    14‚Üí		Output: map[string]interface{}{
    15‚Üí			"output": "ok",
    16‚Üí		},
    17‚Üí	}
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí	out := formatToolAsMarkdown(tool)
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí	// Should NOT have wrapper tags (those are added by pkg/markdown)
    22‚Üí	if strings.Contains(out, "<tool-use") {
    23‚Üí		t.Fatalf("should not contain <tool-use> wrapper tags: %s", out)
    24‚Üí	}
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí	// Should have bash code fence with command
    27‚Üí	if !strings.Contains(out, "```bash") {
    28‚Üí		t.Fatalf("expected bash code fence: %s", out)
    29‚Üí	}
    30‚Üí	if !strings.Contains(out, "ls -la") {
    31‚Üí		t.Fatalf("expected command in output: %s", out)
    32‚Üí	}
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí	// Should have result
    35‚Üí	if !strings.Contains(out, "Result:") {
    36‚Üí		t.Fatalf("expected Result: prefix: %s", out)
    37‚Üí	}
    38‚Üí}

    40‚Üífunc TestFormatToolAsMarkdownWriteFile(t *testing.T) {

    49‚Üí	}

    60‚Üífunc TestFormatToolAsMarkdownReadFile(t *testing.T) {

     1‚Üí// Package cmd contains CLI command implementations for the SpecStory CLI.
     2‚Üípackage cmd
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üíimport (
     5‚Üí	"errors"
     6‚Üí	"fmt"
     7‚Üí	"log/slog"
     8‚Üí	"strings"
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
    13‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
    14‚Üí	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
    15‚Üí)
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí// CreateCheckCommand dynamically creates the check command with provider information.
    18‚Üífunc CreateCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
    19‚Üí	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
    20‚Üí	ids := registry.ListIDs()
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí	// Build dynamic examples
    23‚Üí	var examplesBuilder strings.Builder
    24‚Üí	examplesBuilder.WriteString(`
    25‚Üí# Check all coding agents
    26‚Üíspecstory check`)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí	if len(ids) > 0 {
    29‚Üí		examplesBuilder.WriteString("\n\n# Check specific coding agent")
    30‚Üí		for _, id := range ids {
    31‚Üí			fmt.Fprintf(&examplesBuilder, "\nspecstory check %s", id)
    32‚Üí		}
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí		// Use first provider for custom command example
    35‚Üí		fmt.Fprintf(&examplesBuilder, "\n\n# Check a specific coding agent with a custom command\nspecstory check %s -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\"", ids[0])
    36‚Üí	}
    37‚Üí	examples := examplesBuilder.String()
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí	cmd := &cobra.Command{
    40‚Üí		Use:   "check [provider-id]",
    41‚Üí		Short: "Check if terminal coding agents are properly installed",
    42‚Üí		Long: `Check if terminal coding agents are properly installed and can be invoked by SpecStory.
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíBy default, checks all registered coding agents providers.
    45‚ÜíSpecify a specific agent ID to check only a specific coding agent.`,
    46‚Üí		Example: examples,
    47‚Üí		Args:    cobra.MaximumNArgs(1), // Accept 0 or 1 argument
    48‚Üí		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    49‚Üí			slog.Info("Running in check-install mode")
    50‚Üí			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí			// Get custom command if provided via flag
    53‚Üí			customCmd, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("command")
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí			// Validate that -c flag requires a provider
    56‚Üí			if customCmd != "" && len(args) == 0 {
    57‚Üí				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
    58‚Üí				ids := registry.ListIDs()
    59‚Üí				example := "specstory check <provider> -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\""
    60‚Üí				if len(ids) > 0 {
    61‚Üí					example = fmt.Sprintf("specstory check %s -c \"/custom/path/to/agent\"", ids[0])
    62‚Üí				}
    63‚Üí				return utils.ValidationError{
    64‚Üí					Message: "The -c/--command flag requires a provider to be specified.\n" +
    65‚Üí						"Example: " + example,
    66‚Üí				}
    67‚Üí			}
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí			if len(args) == 0 {
    70‚Üí				// Check all providers
    71‚Üí				return checkAllProviders(registry)
    72‚Üí			} else {
    73‚Üí				// Check specific provider
    74‚Üí				return checkSingleProvider(registry, args[0], customCmd)
    75‚Üí			}
    76‚Üí		},
    77‚Üí	}
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí	cmd.Flags().StringP("command", "c", "", "custom agent execution command for the provider")
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí	return cmd
    82‚Üí}
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí// printDivider prints a divider line for visual separation
    85‚Üífunc printDivider() {
    86‚Üí	fmt.Println("\n--------")
    87‚Üí}
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí// checkSingleProvider checks a specific provider
    90‚Üífunc checkSingleProvider(registry *factory.Registry, providerID, customCmd string) error {
    91‚Üí	provider, err := registry.Get(providerID)
    92‚Üí	if err != nil {
    93‚Üí		// Provider not found - show helpful error
    94‚Üí		fmt.Printf("‚ùå Provider '%s' is not a valid provider implementation\n\n", providerID)
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí		ids := registry.ListIDs()
    97‚Üí		if len(ids) > 0 {
    98‚Üí			fmt.Println("The registered providers are:")
    99‚Üí			for _, id := range ids {
   100‚Üí				if p, _ := registry.Get(id); p != nil {
   101‚Üí					fmt.Printf("  ‚Ä¢ %s - %s\n", id, p.Name())
   102‚Üí				}
   103‚Üí			}
   104‚Üí			fmt.Println("\nExample: specstory check " + ids[0])
   105‚Üí		}
   106‚Üí		return err
   107‚Üí	}
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí	// Set the agent provider for analytics
   110‚Üí	analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{provider.Name()})
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí	// Run the check
   113‚Üí	result := provider.Check(customCmd)
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí	// Display results with the nice formatting
   116‚Üí	if result.Success {
   117‚Üí		fmt.Printf("\n‚ú® %s is installed and ready! ‚ú®\n\n", provider.Name())
   118‚Üí		fmt.Printf("  üì¶ Version: %s\n", result.Version)
   119‚Üí		fmt.Printf("  üìç Location: %s\n", result.Location)
   120‚Üí		fmt.Printf("  ‚úÖ Status: All systems go!\n\n")
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üí		fmt.Println("üöÄ Ready to sync your sessions! üí™")
   123‚Üí		normalizedID := strings.ToLower(providerID)
   124‚Üí		fmt.Printf("   ‚Ä¢ specstory run %s\n", normalizedID)
   125‚Üí		fmt.Println("   ‚Ä¢ specstory sync - Save markdown files for existing sessions")
   126‚Üí		fmt.Println()
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí		return nil
   129‚Üí	} else {
   130‚Üí		fmt.Printf("\n‚ùå %s check failed!\n", provider.Name())
   131‚Üí		if result.ErrorMessage != "" {
   132‚Üí			fmt.Printf("\n%s\n", result.ErrorMessage)
   133‚Üí		}
   134‚Üí		return errors.New("check failed")
   135‚Üí	}
   136‚Üí}
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí// checkAllProviders checks all registered providers
   139‚Üífunc checkAllProviders(registry *factory.Registry) error {
   140‚Üí	// Sort for consistent output
   141‚Üí	ids := registry.ListIDs()
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí	// Collect all provider names for analytics
   144‚Üí	var providerNames []string
   145‚Üí	for _, id := range ids {
   146‚Üí		if provider, err := registry.Get(id); err == nil {
   147‚Üí			providerNames = append(providerNames, provider.Name())
   148‚Üí		}
   149‚Üí	}
   150‚Üí	analytics.SetAgentProviders(providerNames)
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí	anySuccess := false
   153‚Üí	type providerInfo struct {
   154‚Üí		id   string
   155‚Üí		name string
   156‚Üí	}
   157‚Üí	var successfulProviders []providerInfo
   158‚Üí	first := true
   159‚Üí
   160‚Üí	for _, id := range ids {
   161‚Üí		provider, _ := registry.Get(id)
   162‚Üí		// Invoke Check() here to keep registry limited to registration/lookup
   163‚Üí		result := provider.Check("")
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí		// Add divider between providers (but not before the first one)
   166‚Üí		if !first {
   167‚Üí			printDivider()
   168‚Üí		}
   169‚Üí		first = false
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí		if result.Success {
   172‚Üí			anySuccess = true
   173‚Üí			successfulProviders = append(successfulProviders, providerInfo{id: id, name: provider.Name()})
   174‚Üí			fmt.Printf("\n‚ú® %s is installed and ready! ‚ú®\n\n", provider.Name())
   175‚Üí			fmt.Printf("  üì¶ Version: %s\n", result.Version)
   176‚Üí			fmt.Printf("  üìç Location: %s\n", result.Location)
   177‚Üí			fmt.Printf("  ‚úÖ Status: All systems go!\n")
   178‚Üí		} else {
   179‚Üí			fmt.Printf("\n‚ùå %s check failed!\n\n", provider.Name())
   180‚Üí			if result.ErrorMessage != "" {
   181‚Üí				// Show just first line of error for summary view
   182‚Üí				lines := strings.Split(result.ErrorMessage, "\n")
   183‚Üí				fmt.Printf("  Error: %s\n", strings.TrimSpace(lines[0]))
   184‚Üí			}
   185‚Üí		}
   186‚Üí	}
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí	// Show ready message if at least one provider is working
   189‚Üí	if anySuccess {
   190‚Üí		printDivider()
   191‚Üí		fmt.Println("\nüöÄ Ready to sync your sessions! üí™")
   192‚Üí		for _, info := range successfulProviders {
   193‚Üí			fmt.Printf("   ‚Ä¢ specstory run %s\n", info.id)
   194‚Üí		}
   195‚Üí		fmt.Println("   ‚Ä¢ specstory sync - Save markdown files for existing sessions")
   196‚Üí		fmt.Println()
   197‚Üí	} else {
   198‚Üí		printDivider()
   199‚Üí		fmt.Println("\n‚öÝÔ∏è  No providers are currently available")
   200‚Üí		fmt.Println("   Install at least one provider to use SpecStory")
   201‚Üí		fmt.Println("\nüí° Tip: Use 'specstory check <provider>' for detailed installation help")
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí		// Try to show an example with the first registered provider ID
   204‚Üí		ids := registry.ListIDs()
   205‚Üí		if len(ids) > 0 {
   206‚Üí			fmt.Printf("   Example: specstory check %s\n", ids[0])
   207‚Üí		} else {
   208‚Üí			fmt.Println("   Example: specstory check <provider>")
   209‚Üí		}
   210‚Üí	}
   211‚Üí
   212‚Üí	// Return error only if ALL providers failed
   213‚Üí	if !anySuccess {
   214‚Üí		return errors.New("check failed")
   215‚Üí	}
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí	return nil
   218‚Üí}
   219‚Üí

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```go
package session

import (
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"time"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/cloud"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
)

// ValidateSessionData runs schema validation on SessionData when in debug mode.
// Validation is only performed when debugRaw is true to avoid overhead in normal operation.
// Returns true if validation passed or was skipped, false if validation failed.
func ValidateSessionData(session *spi.AgentChatSession, debugRaw bool) bool {
	if !debugRaw || session.SessionData == nil {
		return true
	}
	if !session.SessionData.Validate() {
		slog.Warn("SessionData failed schema validation, proceeding anyway",
			"sessionId", session.SessionID)
		return false
	}
	return true
}

// WriteDebugSessionData writes debug session data when debugRaw is enabled.
// Logs warnings on failure but does not fail the operation.
func WriteDebugSessionData(session *spi.AgentChatSession, debugRaw bool) {
	if !debugRaw || session.SessionData == nil {
		return
	}
	if err := spi.WriteDebugSessionData(session.SessionID, session.SessionData); err != nil {
		slog.Warn("Failed to write debug session data", "sessionId", session.SessionID, "error", err)
	}
}

// FormatFilenameTimestamp formats a timestamp for use in filenames.
// The format is filesystem-safe and matches the markdown title format.
func FormatFilenameTimestamp(t time.Time, useUTC bool) string {
	if useUTC {
		// Use UTC with Z suffix: "2006-01-02_15-04-05Z"
		return t.UTC().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05") + "Z"
	}
	// Use local timezone with offset: "2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700"
	return t.Local().Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700")
}

// BuildSessionFilePath constructs the full markdown file path for a session.
// All callers need consistent filename generation from session metadata.
func BuildSessionFilePath(session *spi.AgentChatSession, historyDir string, useUTC bool) string {
	timestamp, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.CreatedAt)
	timestampStr := FormatFilenameTimestamp(timestamp, useUTC)

	filename := timestampStr
	if session.Slug != "" {
		filename = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", timestampStr, session.Slug)
	}
	return filepath.Join(historyDir, filename+".md")
}

// ProcessSingleSession writes markdown and triggers cloud sync for a single session.
// onlyCloudSync skips local markdown writes and only syncs to cloud.
// isAutosave indicates if this is being called from run/watch (true) or sync command (false).
// debugRaw enables schema validation (only run in debug mode to avoid overhead).
// useUTC controls timestamp format (true=UTC, false=local).
// Returns the size of the markdown content in bytes.
func ProcessSingleSession(session *spi.AgentChatSession, config utils.OutputConfig, onlyCloudSync bool, showOutput bool, isAutosave bool, debugRaw bool, useUTC bool) (int, error) {
	ValidateSessionData(session, debugRaw)
	WriteDebugSessionData(session, debugRaw)

	// Generate markdown from SessionData
	markdownContent, err := GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(session.SessionData, false, useUTC)
	if err != nil {
		slog.Error("Failed to generate markdown from SessionData", "sessionId", session.SessionID, "error", err)
		return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate markdown: %w", err)
	}

	// Calculate markdown size in bytes
	markdownSize := len(markdownContent)

	// Generate filename from timestamp and slug
	fileFullPath := BuildSessionFilePath(session, config.GetHistoryDir(), useUTC)

	if showOutput && !log.IsSilent() {
		fmt.Printf("Processing session %s...", session.SessionID)
	}

	// Check if file already exists with same content
	var outcome string
	identicalContent := false
	fileExists := false
	if existingContent, err := os.ReadFile(fileFullPath); err == nil {
		fileExists = true
		if string(existingContent) == markdownContent {
			identicalContent = true
			slog.Info("Markdown file already exists with same content, skipping write",
				"sessionId", session.SessionID,
				"path", fileFullPath)
		}
	}

	// Write file if needed (skip if only-cloud-sync is enabled)
	if !onlyCloudSync {
		if !identicalContent {
			// Ensure history directory exists (handles deletion during long-running watch/run)
			if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(config); err != nil {
				return 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to ensure history directory: %w", err)
			}
			err := os.WriteFile(fileFullPath, []byte(markdownContent), 0644)
			if err != nil {
				// Track write error
				if isAutosave {
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveError, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"error":           err.Error(),
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				} else {
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownError, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"error":           err.Error(),
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				}
				return 0, fmt.Errorf("error writing markdown file: %w", err)
			}

			// Track successful write
			if isAutosave {
				if !fileExists {
					// New file created during autosave
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveNew, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				} else {
					// File updated during autosave
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventAutosaveSuccess, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				}
			} else {
				if !fileExists {
					// New file created during manual sync
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownNew, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				} else {
					// File updated during manual sync
					analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventSyncMarkdownSuccess, analytics.Properties{
						"session_id":      session.SessionID,
						"only_cloud_sync": onlyCloudSync,
					})
				}
			}

			slog.Info("Successfully wrote file",
				"sessionId", session.SessionID,
				"path", fileFullPath)
		}

		// Determine outcome for user feedback
		if identicalContent {
			outcome = "up to date (skipped)"
		} else if fileExists {
			outcome = "updated"
		} else {
			outcome = "created"
		}
	} else {
		// Only cloud sync mode - no local file operations
		outcome = "synced to cloud only"
		slog.Info("Skipping local file write (only-cloud-sync mode)",
			"sessionId", session.SessionID)
	}

	// Trigger cloud sync with provider-specific data
	// In only-cloud-sync mode: always sync (no file to check for identical content)
	// In normal mode: skip sync only if identical content AND in autosave mode
	if onlyCloudSync || !identicalContent || !isAutosave {
		cloud.SyncSessionToCloud(session.SessionID, fileFullPath, markdownContent, []byte(session.RawData), session.SessionData.Provider.Name, isAutosave)
	}

	if showOutput && !log.IsSilent() {
		fmt.Printf(" %s\n", outcome)
		fmt.Println() // Visual separation
	}

	return markdownSize, nil
}

```

```

  1113‚Üí	if useUTC {

  1119‚Üí}

    91‚Üífunc TestFormatFilenameTimestamp(t *testing.T) {
    92‚Üí	// Create a fixed timezone for predictable UTC conversion tests
    93‚Üí	fixedZone := time.FixedZone("TEST", -7*60*60) // UTC-7
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí	tests := []struct {
    96‚Üí		name     string
    97‚Üí		time     time.Time
    98‚Üí		useUTC   bool
    99‚Üí		expected string
   100‚Üí	}{
   101‚Üí		{
   102‚Üí			name:     "UTC format - standard time",
   103‚Üí			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 15, 30, 45, 0, time.UTC),
   104‚Üí			useUTC:   true,
   105‚Üí			expected: "2026-01-25_15-30-45Z",
   106‚Üí		},
   107‚Üí		{
   108‚Üí			name:     "UTC format - midnight",
   109‚Üí			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
   110‚Üí			useUTC:   true,
   111‚Üí			expected: "2026-01-25_00-00-00Z",
   112‚Üí		},
   113‚Üí		{
   114‚Üí			name:     "UTC format - end of day",
   115‚Üí			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 23, 59, 59, 0, time.UTC),
   116‚Üí			useUTC:   true,
   117‚Üí			expected: "2026-01-25_23-59-59Z",
   118‚Üí		},
   119‚Üí		{
   120‚Üí			name:     "UTC format - time in non-UTC zone gets converted",
   121‚Üí			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 8, 30, 45, 0, fixedZone), // 8:30 in UTC-7 = 15:30 UTC
   122‚Üí			useUTC:   true,
   123‚Üí			expected: "2026-01-25_15-30-45Z",
   124‚Üí		},
   125‚Üí		{
   126‚Üí			name:     "UTC format - leap year date",
   127‚Üí			time:     time.Date(2024, 2, 29, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
   128‚Üí			useUTC:   true,
   129‚Üí			expected: "2024-02-29_12-00-00Z",
   130‚Üí		},
   131‚Üí	}
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí	for _, tt := range tests {
   134‚Üí		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   135‚Üí			result := formatFilenameTimestamp(tt.time, tt.useUTC)
   136‚Üí			if result != tt.expected {
   137‚Üí				t.Errorf("formatFilenameTimestamp() = %q, want %q", result, tt.expected)
   138‚Üí			}
   139‚Üí		})
   140‚Üí	}
   141‚Üí}

package session

import (
	"testing"
	"time"
)

func TestFormatFilenameTimestamp(t *testing.T) {
	// Create a fixed timezone for predictable UTC conversion tests
	fixedZone := time.FixedZone("TEST", -7*60*60) // UTC-7

	tests := []struct {
		name     string
		time     time.Time
		useUTC   bool
		expected string
	}{
		{
			name:     "UTC format - standard time",
			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 15, 30, 45, 0, time.UTC),
			useUTC:   true,
			expected: "2026-01-25_15-30-45Z",
		},
		{
			name:     "UTC format - midnight",
			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
			useUTC:   true,
			expected: "2026-01-25_00-00-00Z",
		},
		{
			name:     "UTC format - end of day",
			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 23, 59, 59, 0, time.UTC),
			useUTC:   true,
			expected: "2026-01-25_23-59-59Z",
		},
		{
			name:     "UTC format - time in non-UTC zone gets converted",
			time:     time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 8, 30, 45, 0, fixedZone), // 8:30 in UTC-7 = 15:30 UTC
			useUTC:   true,
			expected: "2026-01-25_15-30-45Z",
		},
		{
			name:     "UTC format - leap year date",
			time:     time.Date(2024, 2, 29, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
			useUTC:   true,
			expected: "2024-02-29_12-00-00Z",
		},
	}

	for _, tt := range tests {
		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			result := FormatFilenameTimestamp(tt.time, tt.useUTC)
			if result != tt.expected {
				t.Errorf("FormatFilenameTimestamp() = %q, want %q", result, tt.expected)
			}
		})
	}
}

func TestFormatFilenameTimestamp_LocalTimezone(t *testing.T) {
	// Compute expected output using the same logic as the function under test.
	// This makes the test deterministic regardless of machine timezone.
	testTime := time.Date(2026, 1, 25, 15, 30, 45, 0, time.UTC)
	localTime := testTime.Local()
	expected := localTime.Format("2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700")

	result := FormatFilenameTimestamp(testTime, false)

	if result != expected {
		t.Errorf("FormatFilenameTimestamp() = %q, want %q", result, expected)
	}
}

```go
package provenance

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
)

// StartEngine creates and returns a provenance engine if enabled.
// Returns nil engine and no-op cleanup if provenance is not enabled.
// The caller must invoke the returned cleanup function (typically via defer).
func StartEngine(enabled bool) (*Engine, func(), error) {
	if !enabled {
		return nil, func() {}, nil
	}

	engine, err := NewEngine()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance engine: %w", err)
	}

	cleanup := func() {
		if closeErr := engine.Close(); closeErr != nil {
			slog.Error("Failed to close provenance engine", "error", closeErr)
		}
		slog.Info("Provenance engine stopped")
	}

	return engine, cleanup, nil
}

// StartFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher that pushes FileEvents
// to the provenance engine for correlation with agent activity. Returns a cleanup
// function the caller must invoke (typically via defer). If the engine is nil
// (provenance disabled), returns a no-op cleanup.
func StartFSWatcher(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, rootDir string) (func(), error) {
	if engine == nil {
		return func() {}, nil
	}

	watcher, err := NewFSWatcher(engine, rootDir)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance FS watcher: %w", err)
	}

	watcher.Start(ctx)
	return watcher.Stop, nil
}

// ProcessEvents extracts agent events from the session and pushes them
// to the provenance engine. Safe to call with nil engine (no-op).
func ProcessEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
	if engine == nil || session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
		return
	}
	ProcessSessionEvents(ctx, engine, session.SessionData)
}

```

    59‚Üí// startProvenanceEngine creates and returns a provenance engine if --provenance is enabled.
    60‚Üí// Returns nil engine and nil cleanup if provenance is not enabled.
    61‚Üí// The caller must invoke the returned cleanup function (typically via defer).
    62‚Üífunc startProvenanceEngine() (*provenance.Engine, func(), error) {
    63‚Üí	if !provenanceEnabled {
    64‚Üí		return nil, func() {}, nil
    65‚Üí	}
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí	engine, err := provenance.NewEngine()
    68‚Üí	if err != nil {
    69‚Üí		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance engine: %w", err)
    70‚Üí	}
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí	cleanup := func() {
    73‚Üí		if closeErr := engine.Close(); closeErr != nil {
    74‚Üí			slog.Error("Failed to close provenance engine", "error", closeErr)
    75‚Üí		}
    76‚Üí		slog.Info("Provenance engine stopped")
    77‚Üí	}
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí	return engine, cleanup, nil
    80‚Üí}
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí// startProvenanceFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher that pushes FileEvents
    83‚Üí// to the provenance engine for correlation with agent activity. Returns a cleanup
    84‚Üí// function the caller must invoke (typically via defer). If the engine is nil
    85‚Üí// (provenance disabled), returns a no-op cleanup.
    86‚Üífunc startProvenanceFSWatcher(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, rootDir string) (func(), error) {
    87‚Üí	if engine == nil {
    88‚Üí		return func() {}, nil
    89‚Üí	}
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí	watcher, err := provenance.NewFSWatcher(engine, rootDir)
    92‚Üí	if err != nil {
    93‚Üí		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance FS watcher: %w", err)
    94‚Üí	}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí	watcher.Start(ctx)
    97‚Üí	return watcher.Stop, nil
    98‚Üí}
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí// processProvenanceEvents extracts agent events from the session and pushes them
   101‚Üí// to the provenance engine. Safe to call with nil engine (no-op).
   102‚Üífunc processProvenanceEvents(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   103‚Üí	if engine == nil || session == nil || session.SessionData == nil {
   104‚Üí		return
   105‚Üí	}
   106‚Üí	provenance.ProcessSessionEvents(ctx, engine, session.SessionData)
   107‚Üí}
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí// UUID regex pattern: 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal characters
   110‚Üívar uuidRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$`)
   111‚Üí

```go
package cmd

import (
	"log/slog"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/cloud"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
)

// CheckAndWarnAuthentication warns the user if cloud sync is enabled but authentication
// is missing or has failed. Uses log.IsSilent() to respect silent mode.
func CheckAndWarnAuthentication(noCloudSync bool) {
	if !noCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() && !log.IsSilent() {
		// Check if this was due to a 401 authentication failure
		if cloud.HadAuthFailure() {
			// Show the specific message for auth failures with orange warning and emoji
			slog.Warn("Cloud sync authentication failed (401)")
			log.UserWarn("‚öÝÔ∏è Unable to authenticate with SpecStory Cloud. This could be due to revoked or expired credentials, or network/server issues.\n")
			log.UserMessage("‚ÑπÔ∏è If this persists, run `specstory logout` then `specstory login` to reset your SpecStory Cloud authentication.\n")
		} else {
			// Regular "not authenticated" message
			msg := "‚öÝÔ∏è Cloud sync not available. You're not authenticated."
			slog.Warn(msg)
			log.UserWarn("%s\n", msg)
			log.UserMessage("‚ÑπÔ∏è Use `specstory login` to authenticate, or `--no-cloud-sync` to skip this warning.\n")
		}
	}
}

```

   615‚Üí			// Track sessions we've seen to suppress initial-scan output.

   618‚Üí			var seenMu sync.Mutex

   621‚Üí			// Session callback for watch mode output

   645‚Üí				// New sessions (!fileExists) always get output since they represent real activity.
   646‚Üí				seenMu.Lock()
   647‚Üí				firstSeen := !seenSessions[session.SessionID]
   648‚Üí				seenSessions[session.SessionID] = true

   650‚Üí				if firstSeen && fileExists {

   652‚Üí				}

   654‚Üí				// Output formatted line to stdout for watch mode
   655‚Üí				if !silent {

   662‚Üí					// Get timestamps from session data
   663‚Üí					startTime := session.CreatedAt

   667‚Üí					}

   694‚Üí							"total_user_prompts": userPrompts,

   696‚Üí						}

   701‚Üí					} else {

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"strings"
	gosync "sync"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/cloud"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/log"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/provenance"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/session"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
)

// truncateSessionID shortens a UUID to first5...last5 for display.
// Full IDs are available in --json output; the short form is enough
// to visually distinguish sessions.
func truncateSessionID(id string) string {
	if len(id) <= 13 {
		return id
	}
	return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
}

// CreateWatchCommand dynamically creates the watch command with provider information.
func CreateWatchCommand() *cobra.Command {
	registry := factory.GetRegistry()
	ids := registry.ListIDs()
	providerList := registry.GetProviderList()

	// Build dynamic examples
	examples := `
# Watch all registered agent providers for activity
specstory watch`

	if len(ids) > 0 {
		examples += "\n\n# Watch for activity from a specific agent"
		for _, id := range ids {
			examples += fmt.Sprintf("\nspecstory watch %s", id)
		}
	}

	examples += `

# Watch with custom output directory
specstory watch --output-dir ~/my-sessions`

	longDesc := `Watch for coding agent activity in the current directory and auto-save markdown files.

Unlike 'run', this command does not launch a coding agent - it only monitors for agent activity.
Use this when you want to run the agent separately, but still want auto-saved markdown files.

By default, 'watch' is for activity from all registered agent providers. Specify a specific agent ID to watch for activity from only that agent.`
	if providerList != "No providers registered" {
		longDesc += "\n\nAvailable provider IDs: " + providerList + "."
	}

	watchCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:     "watch [provider-id]",
		Aliases: []string{"w"},
		Short:   "Watch for coding agent activity with auto-save",
		Long:    longDesc,
		Example: examples,
		Args:    cobra.MaximumNArgs(1), // Accept 0 or 1 argument (provider ID)
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			slog.Info("Running in watch mode")

			registry := factory.GetRegistry()

			// Read flags from the command
			debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
			useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
			useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
			jsonOutput, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
			outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
			noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
			onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
			provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")

			// Setup output configuration
			config, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(outputDir)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			// Ensure history directory exists for watch mode
			if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(config); err != nil {
				return err
			}

			// Initialize project identity (needed for cloud sync)
			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
			if err != nil {
				slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
				return err
			}
			if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
				// Log error but don't fail the command
				slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
			}

			// Check authentication for cloud sync
			CheckAndWarnAuthentication(noCloudSync)

			// Validate that --only-cloud-sync requires authentication
			if onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
				return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
			}

			// Start provenance engine if enabled (used in later phases for event correlation)
			provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(provenanceEnabled)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			defer provenanceCleanup()

			providerIDs := registry.ListIDs()
			if len(providerIDs) == 0 {
				return fmt.Errorf("no providers registered")
			}
			if len(args) > 0 {
				providerIDs = []string{args[0]}
			}

			// Collect provider names for analytics
			providers := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
			for _, id := range providerIDs {
				if provider, err := registry.Get(id); err == nil {
					providers[id] = provider
				} else {
					return fmt.Errorf("no provider %s found", id)
				}
			}
			var providerNames []string
			// Get all provider names from the providers map
			for _, provider := range providers {
				providerNames = append(providerNames, provider.Name())
			}
			analytics.SetAgentProviders(providerNames)

			// Track watch command activation
			analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventWatchActivated, nil)

			// Create context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C handling)
			// This allows providers to clean up resources when user presses Ctrl+C
			ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
			defer cancel()

			// Start filesystem watcher for provenance correlation if enabled (uses signal context for Ctrl+C)
			fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			defer fsCleanup()

			if !log.IsSilent() && !jsonOutput {
				fmt.Println()
				agentWord := "agents"
				if len(providerNames) == 1 {
					agentWord = "agent"
				}
				fmt.Println("üëÄ Watching for activity from " + agentWord + ": " + strings.Join(providerNames, ", "))
				fmt.Println("   Press Ctrl+C to stop watching")
				fmt.Println()
			}

			// Track sessions we've seen to suppress initial-scan output.
			// Existing sessions found at startup get their markdown refreshed
			// but don't produce output ‚Äî only new activity does.
			var seenMu gosync.Mutex
			seenSessions := make(map[string]bool)

			// Session callback for watch mode output
			sessionCallback := func(providerID string, sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
				// Check if markdown file already exists to determine if this is an update or creation
				fileFullPath := session.BuildSessionFilePath(sess, config.GetHistoryDir(), useUTC)
				_, fileExistsErr := os.Stat(fileFullPath)
				fileExists := fileExistsErr == nil

				// Process the session (write markdown and sync to cloud)
				// Don't show output during watch mode
				// This is autosave mode (true)
				markdownSize, err := session.ProcessSingleSession(sess, config, onlyCloudSync, false, true, debugRaw, useUTC)
				if err != nil {
					// Log error but continue - don't fail the whole watch
					// In watch mode, we prioritize keeping the watcher running.
					// Failed markdown writes or cloud syncs can be retried later via
					// the sync command, so we just log and continue.
					slog.Error("Failed to process session update",
						"sessionId", sess.SessionID,
						"provider", providerID,
						"error", err)
					return
				}

				// Suppress output for existing sessions seen for the first time (initial scan).
				// New sessions (!fileExists) always get output since they represent real activity.
				seenMu.Lock()
				firstSeen := !seenSessions[sess.SessionID]
				seenSessions[sess.SessionID] = true
				seenMu.Unlock()
				if firstSeen && fileExists {
					return
				}

				// Output formatted line to stdout for watch mode
				if !log.IsSilent() {
					// Determine if this was an update or creation
					action := "updated"
					if !fileExists {
						action = "created"
					}

					// Get timestamps from session data
					startTime := sess.CreatedAt
					endTime := startTime
					if sess.SessionData != nil && sess.SessionData.UpdatedAt != "" {
						endTime = sess.SessionData.UpdatedAt
					}

					// Count messages by role
					userPrompts := 0
					agentActivity := 0
					if sess.SessionData != nil {
						for _, exchange := range sess.SessionData.Exchanges {
							for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
								if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {
									userPrompts++
								} else {
									agentActivity++
								}
							}
						}
					}

					// Output the formatted line
					if jsonOutput {
						record := map[string]interface{}{
							"timestamp":          time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
							"action":             action,
							"session_id":         sess.SessionID,
							"start_time":         startTime,
							"end_time":           endTime,
							"provider":           providerID,
							"markdown_size":      markdownSize,
							"total_user_prompts": userPrompts,
							"agent_activity":     agentActivity,
						}
						if !onlyCloudSync {
							record["markdown_file"] = fileFullPath
						}
						_ = json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout).Encode(record)
					} else {
						emoji := "‚ôªÔ∏è"
						if action == "created" {
							emoji = "‚ú®"
						}
						activityWord := "activities"
						if agentActivity == 1 {
							activityWord = "activity"
						}
						promptWord := "prompts"
						if userPrompts == 1 {
							promptWord = "prompt"
						}
						fmt.Printf("  %s  %s  %s ¬∑ %s ¬∑ %d %s ¬∑ %d agent %s\n",
							time.Now().Format("15:04:05"),
							emoji,
							providerID,
							truncateSessionID(sess.SessionID),
							userPrompts,
							promptWord,
							agentActivity,
							activityWord)
					}
				}

				// Push agent events to provenance engine for correlation
				provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
			}

			return utils.WatchProviders(ctx, cwd, providers, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
		},
	}

	// Watch-specific flags
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "output session updates as JSON lines (one JSON object per line)")
	watchCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
	_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw") // Hidden flag
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", false, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
	watchCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
	_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance") // Hidden flag
	watchCmd.Flags().String("cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
	_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag

	return watchCmd
}

       |_|                                  |___/ 


  Watch for coding agent activity in the current directory and auto-save markdown files.                                
                                                                                                                        
  Unlike 'run', this command does not launch a coding agent - it only monitors for agent activity.                      
  Use this when you want to run the agent separately, but still want auto-saved markdown files.                         
                                                                                                                        
  By default, 'watch' is for activity from all registered agent providers. Specify a specific agent ID to watch for     
  activity from only that agent.                                                                                        
                                                                                                                        
  Available provider IDs: claude (Claude Code), codex (Codex CLI), cursor (Cursor CLI), droid (Factory Droid CLI),      
  gemini (Gemini CLI).                                                                                                  
         
  USAGE  
         
    specstory watch [provider-id] [--flags]              
            
  EXAMPLES  
            
    # Watch all registered agent providers for activity  
    specstory watch                                      
                                                         
    # Watch for activity from a specific agent           
    specstory watch claude                               
    specstory watch codex                                
    specstory watch cursor                               
    specstory watch droid                                
    specstory watch gemini                               
                                                         
    # Watch with custom output directory                 
    specstory watch --output-dir ~/my-sessions           
         
  FLAGS  
         
    --json             Output session updates as JSON lines (one JSON object per line)
    --local-time-zone  Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)
    --no-cloud-sync    Disable cloud sync functionality
    --only-cloud-sync  Skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)
    --output-dir       Custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-22 13:27:19Z)**_

All 7 steps are complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

### Files changed

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pkg/session/markdown.go` | Renamed from `pkg/markdown/markdown.go`, package `markdown` ‚Üí `session` |
| `pkg/session/markdown_test.go` | Renamed from `pkg/markdown/markdown_test.go`, package `markdown` ‚Üí `session` |
| `pkg/session/session.go` | **New** ‚Äî `ProcessSingleSession`, `BuildSessionFilePath`, `FormatFilenameTimestamp`, `ValidateSessionData`, `WriteDebugSessionData` |
| `pkg/session/session_test.go` | **New** ‚Äî tests for `FormatFilenameTimestamp` (moved from `main_test.go`) |
| `pkg/provenance/provenance.go` | **New** ‚Äî `StartEngine`, `StartFSWatcher`, `ProcessEvents` wrappers |
| `pkg/cmd/utils.go` | **New** ‚Äî `CheckAndWarnAuthentication` |
| `pkg/cmd/watch.go` | **New** ‚Äî `CreateWatchCommand` with `truncateSessionID` helper and all flag registration |
| `pkg/providers/geminicli/markdown_tools.go` | Updated comment reference `pkg/markdown` ‚Üí `pkg/session` |
| `pkg/providers/geminicli/markdown_tools_test.go` | Updated comment reference `pkg/markdown` ‚Üí `pkg/session` |
| `main.go` | Removed moved functions, updated imports (`cmdpkg`/`sessionpkg` aliases to avoid cobra `cmd` shadowing), inlined `getUseUTC`, removed `jsonOutput` var |
| `main_test.go` | Removed `FormatFilenameTimestamp` tests (moved to `pkg/session/session_test.go`), removed unused `time` import |

### Key design decisions

- **Import aliases** (`cmdpkg`, `sessionpkg`) in main.go to avoid name collisions with cobra's `cmd *cobra.Command` parameter and the `session *spi.AgentChatSession` local variable
- **`ProcessSingleSession`** takes `onlyCloudSync` as an explicit parameter and uses `log.IsSilent()` instead of package-level vars
- **Watch flags** are registered inside `CreateWatchCommand()` and read via `cmd.Flags().Get*()` ‚Äî no package-level vars needed
- **`gosync`** import alias in watch.go to avoid collision with Go's `sync` package and the concept of "sync" in the codebase
